name: llm-agrees-with-rule
description: v1.5: when the LLM picks the SAME archetype slot the rule-based scorer would've picked, source must still be 'llm' (the routing layer was consulted and its pick won, even though it matches). This catches a class of future regressions where a refactor accidentally flips source back to 'rule' on agreement, which would silently hide the LLM's contribution in canary rollups. Fact-driven transcript routes to producer; LLM also picks producer; assert sourceIn=['llm'] and rationale flows into reason.

input.transcript: Airbnb was founded in 2008 and raised $20 million in their Series A according to the report. The company went public in December 2020 and has a market cap of roughly 85 billion dollars with over 150 million users worldwide.

llmPick.rationale: transcript is stuffed with verifiable claims — founding year, funding round, market cap, user count. Producer's specialty.
